mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, consumer confidence stabilizes and corporate revenue growth accelerates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate profit margins and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across two consecutive business cycles, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, rising inflation expectations combined with higher policy rates compress equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered tightening episodes, or the combined inflation-rate signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.0 percent directly boosts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month jump in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, which immediately lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative or flat return profile in more than 60% of threshold breaches, or the 5-day forward return spread between triggered and non-triggered periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate validate labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint payroll-unemployment signal fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 20-day SPX returns across two consecutive market cycles, or the interaction coefficient becomes negative and significant.

mechanism_narrative: In credit contraction phases, falling revolving consumer credit combined with declining housing permits signals weakening aggregate demand, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 and RSAFS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where revolving credit falls and housing starts drop below 1000, or the signal's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 4-year test window.
